content:
# elizaos/eliza Monthly Report (June 2026)

## 🚀 Highlights
June 2026 was a transformative month for ElizaOS, centered on the launch of "Eliza Cloud Apps" (Product 2) and the hardening of our cloud infrastructure. The development team successfully transitioned to a per-tenant isolated container hosting model, significantly improving security and scalability for provisioned agents. Alongside these infrastructure milestones, we prioritized developer experience through modular plugin refactoring and UI/UX enhancements, ensuring that the framework remains both robust for enterprise-grade cloud deployments and accessible for local development.

## 🛠️ Key Developments

### Cloud Infrastructure & Eliza Cloud Apps
The primary focus was the deployment of the Eliza Cloud Apps data plane, enabling isolated container hosting with custom URLs and metered billing.
*   **Provisioning & Scaling:** Implemented per-tenant database isolation and established Terraform modules for staging and production environments ([#8293](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza/pull/8293), [#8296](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza/pull/8296)).
*   **Authentication & Security:** Overhauled the Steward OAuth flow by implementing PKCE, request signing, and tenant-level allowlists to resolve production authentication blockers ([#8259](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza/pull/8259), [#8280](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza/pull/8280)).
*   **API & SDK:** Expanded the Cloud API with container lifecycle management verbs and added `ElizaCloudClient.transcribeAudio()` to the Cloud SDK ([#8270](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza/pull/8270), [#8266](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza/pull/8266)).

### Orchestration & Plugin Architecture
We moved toward a more modular ecosystem by decomposing monolithic plugins and refining agent autonomy.
*   **Modularization:** The `plugin-lifeops` monolith was successfully refactored into a lightweight orchestrator and six specialized, domain-focused plugins ([#8277](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza/pull/8277)).
*   **Task Coordination:** The Odysseus task coordinator received significant state-parity fixes, including improvements to `CompareView` and `NotesPanel` ([#8204](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza/pull/8204)).
*   **Agent Autonomy:** Enabled autonomous goal-driven loops for monetized apps with capped self-spend capabilities ([#8250](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza/pull/8250)).

### UI/UX & Developer Experience
*   **Continuous Chat:** Introduced a prompt-suggestion strip and liquid-glass motion effects to the chat overlay, improving onboarding and interactivity ([#8201](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza/pull/8201), [#8238](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza/pull/8238)).
*   **Desktop Optimization:** Implemented tray-first startup and deferred embedding to reduce startup stalls for local users ([#8148](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza/pull/8148)).

## 🐛 Issues & Triage

### Closed Issues
*   **Stability & Routing:** Resolved critical regressions in sub-agent deliverable truncation and build-intent mis-routing ([#7970](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza/issues/7970)).
*   **Voice & Persistence:** Fixed unreachable voice-profile routes, ensuring speaker persistence in the relationship graph ([#8234](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza/issues/8234)).
*   **Ledger Unification:** Resolved "stranded credits" by unifying app-credit purchases and inference spending into a single org-level ledger ([#8253](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza/issues/8253)).

### New & Active Issues
*   **Cloud Scaling:** [#8321](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza/issues/8321) highlights emerging scaling and ingress gaps in the apps data plane that require attention for future stability.
*   **Bundling Strategy:** Ongoing debate regarding `app-core` externalization and `bundleDependencies` continues to be a point of discussion for cross-environment compatibility ([#8142](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza/issues/8142)).

## 💬 Community & Collaboration
June saw high-intensity collaboration, particularly regarding the stabilization of the Steward authentication service and the architectural shift toward modular plugins. The community demonstrated strong consensus on moving away from forced planner-routing for `WEB_FETCH` in favor of sub-agent paths ([#8122](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza/issues/8122)). Additionally, the registry continues to grow with third-party contributions like `@1claw/plugin-elizaos` and `@madeonsol/plugin-madeonsol`, signaling a healthy and expanding ecosystem of external developers building on the ElizaOS framework.
